Technical plan: Map effort, dates, protocols, observers, localities, sections, event quality, and missingness before looking at bird effects.
Lay summary: Shows where the data are strong enough to answer questions and where they are not.

Outcome: numeric, X, lower-bound, heaped, extreme counts
Technical plan: Quantify count availability, rounding/heaping, duplicated shared-checklist disagreements, and upper-tail influence by species and guild.
Lay summary: Checks whether a reported flock of 500 can be compared fairly with a report of X or 5.

Technical plan: Count independent events, checklists, observers, and localities in every proposed time-distance cell before modelling.
Lay summary: Prevents fitting impressive-looking curves where almost no data exist.

Technical plan: Binomial GAMM/GLMM with event time, distance, effort, observer, locality, year, and calendar controls.
Lay summary: Asks whether a species is more likely to appear on a complete checklist near spawn.

Technical plan: Two-part model: probability of detection and flock size conditional on a numeric report.
Lay summary: Separates ‘does it show up?’ from ‘how many are there when it does?’

Technical plan: Negative-binomial, generalized Poisson, or Tweedie benchmark selected by simulation diagnostics.
Lay summary: Provides one overall count response while checking whether one distribution can handle many zeros and huge flocks.

Technical plan: Cumulative-link model robust to heaping and extreme numerical counts.
Lay summary: Tests whether large flocks become more common without pretending every count is exact.

Technical plan: Threshold exceedance, quantile, or generalized Pareto sensitivity for well-supported taxa.
Lay summary: Targets spectacular aggregations that ordinary averages can hide.

Outcome: guild detection and positive total count
Technical plan: Mechanistic guild totals with species contribution audits and leave-one-species-out checks.
Lay summary: Tests whether groups such as roe-diving ducks or gulls respond even when individual species are noisy.

Technical plan: Tweedie/NB/hurdle model with dominant-species decomposition.
Lay summary: Asks the broad question: are more herring-associated birds recorded near spawning events?

Technical plan: Poisson/NB or occupancy-adjusted richness with effort controls.
Lay summary: Tests whether spawn attracts a more diverse bird assemblage, not only more individuals.

Technical plan: Checklist-level and event-area diversity with sample-size and effort standardization.
Lay summary: Distinguishes a genuinely diverse gathering from one giant flock of a single species.

Technical plan: Non-overlapping event-time bins with simultaneous calendar controls and event-cluster uncertainty.
Lay summary: Shows when the response begins, peaks, and fades.

Outcome: smooth response over days from spawn
Technical plan: Restricted low-rank smooth or distributed-lag model inside supported periods.
Lay summary: Avoids forcing the biological response into one arbitrary two-week box.

Technical plan: Repeated imputation or latent date interval sensitivity.
Lay summary: Acknowledges that the recorded date may not be the exact day birds first respond.

Technical plan: Ring-specific event-time effects with support and spatial-precision checks.
Lay summary: Directly answers how close birds need to be to spawn for the signal to appear.

Technical plan: Log-distance smooth or monotonic effect, with protocol-specific spatial precision.
Lay summary: Estimates how rapidly the response weakens with distance rather than choosing only one radius.

Technical plan: Tensor-product GAM or spatial-temporal field restricted to well-supported cells.
Lay summary: Shows whether the response is brief and very local, or broader and longer-lasting.

Outcome: weighted exposure to all concurrent events
Technical plan: Distance-decay kernels summed over overlapping events, with alternative bandwidths.
Lay summary: Handles coastlines where birds may be responding to several nearby spawns at once.

Outcome: point, length-based shoreline segment, event complex
Technical plan: Compare point distance with conservative extent buffers and shoreline-linked exposure.
Lay summary: Checks whether a long spawn was wrongly represented by one point.

Outcome: share of birds in near, middle, and far zones
Technical plan: Multinomial or Dirichlet-multinomial model conditional on the regional observed total.
Lay summary: Tests whether birds are redistributed toward spawn even when total regional numbers stay similar.

Outcome: near count, far count, regional total
Technical plan: Joint or stacked models with common event-day risk sets.
Lay summary: Separates local concentration from a broader regional influx.

Outcome: same locality across exposed and unexposed dates/years
Technical plan: Locality fixed effects, year/calendar controls, and event clustering.
Lay summary: Compares a place with itself, reducing bias from some sites always having more birds.

Technical plan: Observer-stratified conditional models or fixed effects.
Lay summary: Reduces differences caused by some birders being more skilled or more likely to visit spawn.

Technical plan: Event-specific risk sets with pre-trend checks and event-cluster bootstrap.
Lay summary: Asks whether active shorelines changed more than similar shorelines on the same dates.

Technical plan: Weighted donor locations or matrix-completion methods for very well-observed events.
Lay summary: Builds a custom ‘what would have happened without spawn’ comparison for the best-monitored places.

Technical plan: Jaccard, phi, odds ratios, conditional detection, and count correlations by phase and distance.
Lay summary: Identifies which species tend to be reported together, while recognizing that one checklist is not proof of one mixed flock.

Technical plan: Permutation/null models preserving species prevalence, checklist richness, location, date, and effort strata.
Lay summary: Checks whether species occur together more often than expected just because both are common.

Technical plan: Species-specific herring/effort effects plus multiple latent factors and residual association matrices.
Lay summary: Separates shared responses to spawn and habitat from leftover species associations.

Technical plan: Count-capable latent-variable model for a reduced, well-supported species set.
Lay summary: Tests whether flock sizes of different species rise and fall together after adjustment.

Technical plan: Compare pre, active, egg, and post networks and inner/outer distance zones.
Lay summary: Tests whether mixed-species gatherings become more tightly connected during spawn.

Technical plan: GLLVM plus constrained ordination/PERMANOVA as a transparent benchmark.
Lay summary: Asks whether the whole bird community changes, even when total bird numbers do not.

Technical plan: Component-specific modifiers with missingness indicators and method interactions.
Lay summary: Tests whether stronger or more extensive spawn records draw larger responses without treating missing measurements as zero.

Technical plan: Bayesian measurement model or multiple-indicator latent score.
Lay summary: Combines imperfect herring measures while keeping their uncertainty visible.

Technical plan: Repeat core models using source rows and adjacency-based event complexes.
Lay summary: Checks whether one biological spawn was split into several database records.

Technical plan: Spatiotemporal GAM/hierarchical migration model.
Lay summary: Tests whether mobile birds track the northward progression of spawning.

Outcome: annual bird metrics vs current/lagged spawn
Technical plan: Region-year time-series model with effort standardization and autocorrelation.
Lay summary: Looks for broader food-web effects that persist beyond the immediate event.

Technical plan: Descriptive and hierarchical models only where reporting is sufficiently complete.
Lay summary: Explores whether particular age groups or feeding behaviours are especially associated with spawn.

Technical plan: Event-area submission allocation and repeated-observer choice models.
Lay summary: Measures whether birders—not only birds—shift toward active spawn.

Technical plan: Model count availability among detections by exposure, observer, species, and flock size proxies.
Lay summary: Checks whether big spawn flocks are more likely to be reported as X instead of a number.

Technical plan: Model whether conspicuous aggregations change search duration, reporting completeness, or detection of unrelated taxa.
Lay summary: Checks whether seeing a huge flock makes observers notice or report more species overall.

Technical plan: Privacy-safe local text mining for spawn, roe, feeding, and mixed-flock terms; no public quotes or identifiers.
Lay summary: Uses observers’ notes only as supporting evidence and never exposes personal or exact-location information.

Technical plan: Repeat core contrasts at ±14, ±28, and matched random dates.
Lay summary: A true spawn signal should weaken when the event date is deliberately wrong.

Technical plan: Geographically plausible placebo exposures and no-recorded-spawn years.
Lay summary: A true spatial signal should be stronger at real spawn shorelines than at fake ones.

Technical plan: Separate habitat-matched falsification models; never force a triple difference without exchangeability.
Lay summary: Checks for broad seasonal artifacts, while admitting that no control species is perfect.

Technical plan: Leave-event/year/region-out validation, spatial blocks, influence deletion, and posterior predictive checks.
Lay summary: Tests whether conclusions survive new places and years rather than only fitting the data already seen.

Outcome: future eBird releases and new spawn events
Technical plan: Freeze the model now and evaluate later data without changing it.
Lay summary: Provides the strongest future confirmation because Version 2 is designed after seeing Version 1 results.

Technical plan: Second-stage multivariate meta-analysis with traits and cross-species covariance.
Lay summary: Summarizes the average guild response without hiding species that behave differently.

Technical plan: Hierarchical partial pooling or multivariate meta-analysis across stock regions.
Lay summary: Shows whether the relationship is general or mainly a Strait of Georgia phenomenon.

Technical plan: Direction, magnitude, uncertainty, predictive performance, and placebo evidence summarized without averaging incompatible outcomes.
Lay summary: Combines many analyses into one transparent conclusion instead of selecting the most favourable model.
